title: Supreme Court Strategic Delay Before National Election

violations.summary: The Court appears to be using delay itself to affect the political timing of a major constitutional dispute.

bottlenecks.summary: Supreme Court failed to publish a public notice identifying the case and the reason for delay if no decision has issued within six months of argument by day 180 under Article IV Section 10.1.

satisfied_duties.duty: review the pattern of judicial delay and determine whether it reflects strategic misconduct rather than genuine complexity or workload
satisfied_duties.outcome: found that the delay pattern was strategic and intended to achieve political effect rather than attributable to genuine complexity or workload

failed_duties.duty: publish a public notice identifying the case and the reason for delay if no decision has issued within six months of argument
failed_duties.outcome: Supreme Court failed to publish a public notice identifying the case and the reason for delay if no decision has issued within six months of argument by day 180 under Article IV Section 10.1.

ai_summary.primary_violation: The Court appears to be using delay itself to affect the political timing of a major constitutional dispute.
ai_summary.primary_bottleneck: Supreme Court failed to publish a public notice identifying the case and the reason for delay if no decision has issued within six months of argument by day 180 under Article IV Section 10.1.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
